data.title: CVS Health falls on report of new CEO, earnings miss
data.description:
• Shares of CVS Health (NYSE:CVS) lost ~8% in the premarket on Friday after The Wall Street Journal reported a leadership transition and below-consensus Q3 earnings at the pharmacy chain operator.

  • According to the Journal, David Joyner, a longtime CVS executive, could replace the company’s current CEO, Karen Lynch, as early as Friday.
  • CVS (CVS), which is based in Woonsocket, Rhode Island, is also expected to withdraw its most recent full-year earnings guidance and report below-consensus Q3 earnings.
  • Joyner, currently an executive vice president and the head of CVS’ pharmacy-benefit manager, CVS Caremark, is also expected to become the healthcare giant’s president.

This is a developing story. Check back for more updates.

data.title: Samsung delays taking shipments of ASML's chip making machines for new US plant - report
data.description:
Samsung Electronics (OTCPK:SSNLF) has postponed taking deliveries of ASML's (NASDAQ:ASML) chipmaking machine for its upcoming facility in Texas as it has yet to get any major customers for the project, Reuters reported citing people with knowledge of the matter.

The South Korean tech giant has also paused the placing of orders to some other suppliers for the $17B factory in Taylor city, prompting them to look for other customers and sent staff deployed on the site back home, the report added.

The delay in equipment deliveries is another setback to the project, which is at the core of Samsung Chairman Jay Y. Lee's aim to expand beyond its memory chips business into contract chip manufacturing, an area where Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) leads. In the memory business, the market for high bandwidth memory, or HBM, chips is led by South Korean companies SK hynix and Samsung, and to a lesser extent by American chipmaker Micron Technology (MU).

On Thursday, TSM's results assuaged sentiments on AI demand, after ASML's results earlier this week had sent shockwaves through the market.

The delayed shipments to Samsung's factory involves ASML's advanced chipmaking tool known as extreme ultraviolet, or EUV, lithography system, the report added.

The deliveries were scheduled earlier this year, however, the machines are yet to be shipped. Samsung has also pushed back delivery of some ASML equipment to the facility, without disclosing information about the equipment or the revised delivery schedule, according to the report.

It was not known how many EUV machines Samsung had ordered or what were the financial terms.

Samsung noted that there was no change to its plan to begin production of its Taylor fab in 2026, and the return of its personnel was part of a routine rotational shift, the report added.

data.title: Fanhua to change ticker symbol to “AIFU”
data.description:
Fanhua (NASDAQ:FANH) is set to change its ticker symbol from “FANH” to “AIFU”.

The company’s American Depository Shares will begin trading on Nasdaq under the new ticker symbol “AIFU” on October 23, 2024.

The change aligns with the proposed change of the company's English name from “Fanhua Inc.” to “AIX Inc.” as well as its Chinese name, with effect from November 1, 2024, pending approval by its shareholders at the extraordinary general meeting scheduled on October 31, 2024.

data.title: Tesla to face probe by NHTSA over full self-driving crashes
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) will be facing a probe by The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA), after one of the carmaker's vehicles with the full self-driving technology (FSD) struck a pedestrian.

NHTSA has opened a probe into 2.4 million Tesla (TSLA) vehicles.

The U.S. auto safety regulator's Office of Defects Investigation said it has found four reports in which a Tesla (TSLA) vehicle, with FSD engaged, crashed after entering an area of reduced roadway visibility conditions.

In one such crash, the Tesla (TSLA) fatally struck a pedestrian

The ODI has started a preliminary evaluation into the firm's FSD, and will assess the technology's ability to detect and respond appropriately to reduced roadway visibility conditions, any updates or modifications from Tesla (TSLA) to the FSD system that may affect the performance of FSD in reduced roadway visibility conditions, and a few other issues.

Shares of the EV maker were down 0.86%, erasing earlier premarket gains.

data.title: MGP Ingredients stock slides after weak Q3 numbers weigh on full-year outlook
data.description:
MGP Ingredients (NASDAQ:MGPI) has lowered its full-year outlook, citing industry headwinds that are likely to persist at least through the rest of the year.

The spirits producer now expects fiscal 2024 sales of $695M to $705M (vs. prior guidance of $742M to $756M) and adjusted basic earnings per share of $5.55 to $5.65, below prior outlook of $6.12 to $6.23.

“We are disappointed with our third quarter results and fourth quarter outlook. Soft alcohol spirits category trends and elevated industry-wide whiskey inventories are putting greater than expected pressure on our brown goods business with a larger impact on our smaller, craft customer base,” said David Bratcher, CEO and president of MGP Ingredients.

The company expects a 24% Y/Y decline in Q3 sales ($161.5M). Excluding the impact of the Atchison distillery, sales are expected to decline 14%. Q3 adjusted net income is also expected to decline 5%.

MGP Ingredients (MGPI) will report results for the third quarter on October 31, 2024. Shares were down around 20% premarket on Friday.

data.title: Stock index futures higher boosted by Netflix's earnings beat
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Friday, as Netflix's (NFLX) third quarter earnings beat helped bring cheer to investors.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.5% and Dow futures (INDU) were unchanged.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was unchanged at 4.10%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.98%.

Streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) rose 5% in premarket trade after it beat both its top and bottom line estimates in the third quarter. The firm also topped expectations for user growth.

Wall Street on Thursday scaled a fresh all-time intraday high on a boost from technology stocks and a robust retail sales report that bolstered expectations of a soft landing, but then gave up those gains in the afternoon and eventually ended largely flat.

"The main catalyst for the day's market moves was another batch of upbeat U.S. data, which dampened any immediate fears about a potential recession," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

U.S. data once again reminded investors of the wisdom of never underestimating the hedonism of the US consumer. September retail sales were strong, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"ECB yesterday for the first time this cycle. Meanwhile, markets priced out the likelihood of the Federal Reserve doing the same at every meeting over the next few months," Reid added.

The European Central Bank cut its policy rates by 25 basis points, as expected, on Thursday, its third cut of the year.

On the economic side, the housing starts and permits report will land before the bell.

There are also a few Fed speakers, including Fed Atlanta President Raphael Bostic and Fed Minneapolis President Neel Kashkari.
